body.title: [sprint] Fix pre-existing fail: test_rules_engine_add_compiled_rule (compiled rule not selected)
body.description:
**Pre-existing failure** (predates D-domain-object-encapsulation; confirmed on baseline 033039e0 unrelated to loop convergence). tests/scraps/test_routing_compiler.py::test_rules_engine_add_compiled_rule asserts an added compiled rule wins routing (compiled:Platform/S->analyst) but the cost-optimizing selector returns the analyst-fallback (deepseek-v4-flash) instead — the compiled rule is filtered out (likely a difficulty/domain-eligibility mismatch on the compiled RoutingRule vs the analyst request). **Shape:** determine why add_compiled_rule + route() drops the compiled candidate; likely the compiled rule lacks a difficulty/domain the selector requires, or the test builds it with a stale expectation from before the cost-optimizing-router change. **Affected:** unseen_university/devices/inference/rules_engine.py (add_compiled_rule/route), tests/scraps/test_routing_compiler.py. **Test plan:** the test passes (compiled rule wins) OR the test is corrected to the intended post-cost-router behavior with a documented why. Related: D-inference-cost-optimizing-router. Found during T-thin-ds sprint 2026-07-02.

[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
